message:
feat: implement request-aware response interception, StreamBody, and remote abort cancellation

gRPC interceptor (src/grpc/interceptor.rs, src/grpc/server.rs,
tests/grpc_verification.rs):

  Add intercept_response_with_request() to the Interceptor trait,
  allowing interceptors to inspect the originating request when shaping
  responses. The default implementation delegates to intercept_response()
  for backwards compatibility. InterceptorLayer calls the new method in
  reverse layer order (inner-to-outer), matching the response pipeline
  convention.

  MetadataPropagator now implements request-aware response interception:
  it copies whitelisted request metadata keys onto the response if they
  are not already present. The previous intercept_response() remains a
  deliberate no-op since it has no request context.

  Helper copy_metadata_value() handles both Ascii and Binary metadata
  variants. ResponseOrderInterceptor test helper verifies reverse-order
  execution. 5 new tests cover: metadata propagation, requestless noop,
  layer composition, reverse ordering.

HTTP body (src/http/body.rs):

  Implement the Body trait for StreamBody<S> where S: Stream. Previously
  StreamBody was defined but had no Body impl (only a comment about
  avoiding the futures crate). The implementation:
  - Adds a `done` flag for fuse-after-EOF semantics
  - poll_frame() delegates to Stream::poll_next(), sets done on None
  - is_end_stream() checks done flag and stream size_hint
  - size_hint() returns exact(0) when exhausted, default otherwise

  3 new tests: frame polling with trailers + EOF fusing, Pending
  propagation through a two-phase stream, and error propagation from a
  fallible stream.

Remote handle (src/remote.rs):

  abort() now takes &Cx and sends a real CancelRequest message through
  the RemoteRuntime when one is attached. Previously it was a Phase 0
  no-op stub. The method gracefully degrades: if no remote capability or
  runtime is attached, it remains a silent no-op. The cancel reason is
  drawn from the Cx if one exists, falling back to a default
  "remote handle abort" user reason.

  Test added: verifies the CancelRequest envelope is sent with correct
  remote_task_id, origin_node, and reason. Existing no-op test renamed
  to clarify it covers the without-runtime case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
